originalTitle: SanDisk’s new PlayStation 5 SSD will cost you more than three PS5 Pros
summary: SanDisk has announced an expensive way to boost the PlayStation 5's storage capacity. The company's new Optimus GX PRO 850P NVMe SSD is an officially licensed PS5 accessory in capacities ranging from 1TB to 8TB. The largest option can store up to 200 PS5 games (based on average installation sizes) SanDisk claims, but thanks to […]
content: SanDisk has announced an expensive way to boost the PlayStation 5’s storage capacity. The company’s new Optimus GX PRO 850P NVMe SSD is an officially licensed PS5 accessory in capacities ranging from 1TB to 8TB. The largest option can store up to 200 PS5 games (based on average installation sizes) SanDisk claims, but thanks to the global memory shortage, it will cost you $2,959.99 when it’s eventually available through the company’s online store, discounted from $3,699.99. Even after you take into account Sony’s recent console price hikes, you can buy three PS5 Pro consoles for the price of just one of these 8TB sticks. But the smaller capacities are similarly expensive. The 4TB version is $1,499.99 (discounted from $1,874.99), while the 2TB option is $759.99 (down from $949.99) — still $100 more expensive than the standard PS5. The new SSD features an “optimized heatsink built specifically for the PS5 and PS5 Pro M.2 slot” and has been tested for both consoles so gamers can “store more and play more with confidence,” according to SanDisk. A Western Digital SSD (SanDisk’s parent brand) with similar specs to the new $3,000 option is currently available on Amazon for $1,470. And as VGC points out, according to the price comparison site PC Part Picker, that Western Digital SSD sold through retailers like Best Buy and Newegg for around $640 last year. When compared to this new SSD, that’s over a 360 percent price increase for the same capacity. So while AI is potentially making games worse, it’s also making them more expensive to play.
chineseTitle: SanDisk 的新款 PlayStation 5 SSD 价格将超过三台 PS5 Pro
chineseSummary: SanDisk 宣布采用一种昂贵的方法来提高 PlayStation 5 的存储容量。该公司的新款 Optimus GX PRO 850P NVMe SSD 是官方授权的 PS5 配件，容量范围从 1TB 到 8TB。 SanDisk 声称，最大的选项可以存储多达 200 个 PS5 游戏（基于平均安装大小），但这要归功于 [...]
chineseContent: SanDisk 宣布采用一种昂贵的方法来提高 PlayStation 5 的存储容量。该公司的新款 Optimus GX PRO 850P NVMe SSD 是官方授权的 PS5 配件，容量范围从 1TB 到 8TB。 SanDisk 声称，最大的选项可以存储多达 200 个 PS5 游戏（基于平均安装大小），但由于全球内存短缺，最终通过该公司的在线商店购买时，售价为 2,959.99 美元，折扣价为 3,699.99 美元。即使考虑到索尼最近的主机价格上涨，您也可以只用一根 8TB 内存条的价格购买三台 PS5 Pro 主机。但较小的容量同样昂贵。 4TB 版本售价 1,499.99 美元（原价 1,874.99 美元折扣），而 2TB 版本售价 759.99 美元（原价 949.99 美元）——仍然比标准 PS5 贵 100 美元。 SanDisk 表示，新款 SSD 具有“专为 PS5 和 PS5 Pro M.2 插槽打造的优化散热器”，并已针对这两款游戏机进行了测试，因此游戏玩家可以“放心地存储更多内容并玩更多游戏”。一款与 3,000 美元新选项规格相似的 Western Digital SSD（SanDisk 的母品牌）目前在亚马逊上有售，售价为 1,470 美元。 VGC 指出，根据价格比较网站 PC Part Picker 的数据，去年西部数据 SSD 通过百思买和 Newegg 等零售商的售价约为 640 美元。与这款新 SSD 相比，相同容量的价格上涨了 360% 以上。因此，虽然人工智能可能会让游戏变得更糟，但它也会让游戏变得更加昂贵。
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
